title: Bidifferential
summary: Specializes the multilinear framework to two inputs, two weights, and bidifferential tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes the multilinear framework to two inputs, two weights, and bidifferential tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/Bidifferential.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rank-two inputs. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Multi Input 2.
Rank-two input weights. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, its defining expression is the function sending i to if i equals 0 then w₁ else w₂.
correspondence.leanType: def biWeights (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Fin 2 → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Build a rank-two input from two ambient functions. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is the function sending i to if i equals 0 then f else g.
correspondence.leanType: def pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.BiInput

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The left input slot. In explicit terms, for f of type cal conf.bi input, its defining expression is F 0.
The right input slot. In explicit terms, for f of type cal conf.bi input, its defining expression is F 1.

Readable bidifferential tangentiality predicate. In explicit terms, for b of type cal conf.bi operator, and w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights B Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂.
correspondence.leanType: def IsBiTangentialAtWeights (B : CalConf.BiOperator) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Readable bidifferential homogeneity predicate. In explicit terms, for w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, and f of type cal conf.bi input, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Is Multi Homogeneous Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ F.
correspondence.leanType: def IsBiHomogeneous (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (F : CalConf.BiInput) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expected output weight for a bidifferential operator of total degree N. In explicit terms, for w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, and n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Cal Conf.output weight Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ N.
correspondence.leanType: def biOutputWeight (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ 0 equals w₁.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_zero (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 0 = w₁

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ 1 equals w₂.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_one (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 1 = w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.pair Input f g 0 equals f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_zero (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 0 = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.pair Input f g 1 equals g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_one (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 1 = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.left Input Cal Conf.pair Input f g equals f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem leftInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.leftInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.right Input Cal Conf.pair Input f g equals g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem rightInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.rightInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, Cal Conf.total Weight Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ equals w₁ plus w₂.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_biWeights (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight (CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, and N in the natural numbers, Cal Conf.bi Output Weight w₁ w₂ N equals w₁ plus w₂ minus 2 times N.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,w₁,w₂\colon \mathbb{R},\;N\colon \mathbb{N},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{biOutputWeight} w₁ w₂ N = w₁ + w₂ - 2 \cdot N
correspondence.leanType: theorem biOutputWeight_eq (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.biOutputWeight w₁ w₂ N = w₁ + w₂ - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
